purpose: A reusable visual style database for generating distinctive WordPress block themes, style variations, patterns, and theme.json starter tokens.
wordpressTarget: WordPress block themes using theme.json version 3 concepts, with guidance that can be adapted for classic themes.

themeJsonHints: Map palette tokens to settings.color.palette entries with stable slugs.
Map display, body, and accent typography to settings.typography.fontFamilies.
Use layout, spacing, and pattern notes to create templates, template parts, and block patterns.
Keep appearanceTools enabled for exploratory themes, then lock down controls for production theme variants.

styles.name: Brutalist Editorial
styles.summary: A raw, type-led publishing style with stark blocks, exposed structure, and a deliberately uncompromising visual rhythm.

palette.strategy: Near monochrome with one acidic signal color for links, calls to action, and issue labels.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Ink on Newsprint for body copy. Reserve Signal Red for large text, underlines, borders, and filled buttons with Ink text.

typography.display: Condensed grotesk or impact sans stack: Impact, Haettenschweiler, Arial Narrow, sans-serif.
typography.body: Readable sans stack: Arial, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, sans-serif.
typography.accent: Monospace stack: IBM Plex Mono, ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace.
typography.scale: Aggressive fluid scale with very large h1 and tight h2/h3 steps.
typography.usage: Let display type do the visual work; use mono for issue numbers, dates, tags, and metadata.

layout.grid: Twelve-column desktop grid with abrupt two-column article intros and full-width pull quotes.
layout.rhythm: Tight vertical rhythm with generous breaks before major sections.
layout.navigation: Plain text nav in a hard bordered header, no decorative logo lockup required.
layout.hero: Huge title, small deck, and one uncompromising image or flat color block.

imagery.treatment: High contrast, little smoothing, occasional halftone or xerox texture.
imagery.crop: Uncomfortable crops are acceptable when the headline remains dominant.

components.buttons: Rectangular, hard-border, uppercase or mono labels.
components.cards: Article cards use border-only containers or no container, with metadata pinned to the top.
components.forms: Square inputs with thick borders, visible labels, and blunt error states.

themeJsonPresetHints: Disable defaultPalette and register five high-control color presets.
Enable fluid typography and define a display size that can reach poster scale.
Set core/post-title and core/query-title styles to the display family.

accessibility: Avoid long uppercase paragraphs.
Keep line length under 75 characters in article bodies.
Use hover styles that do not rely only on red.

styles.name: Quiet SaaS
styles.summary: A restrained operational interface style for product sites, docs portals, and app-adjacent marketing that needs trust without visual noise.

palette.strategy: Neutral interface base with a measured blue-green primary and amber warning accent.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Graphite text on Canvas or Mist. Teal Signal works for filled controls with white or Canvas text after contrast testing.

typography.display: Modern humanist sans: Inter, Source Sans 3, system-ui, sans-serif.
typography.body: System UI stack for crisp product copy.
typography.accent: Mono stack for metrics, version numbers, and code fragments.
typography.scale: Modest fluid scale with restrained headings and compact labels.
typography.usage: Use weight and spacing instead of decorative type shifts.

layout.grid: Twelve-column grid with narrow copy columns and structured product panels.
layout.rhythm: Consistent 8px spacing logic with larger section bands.
layout.navigation: Sticky product nav with compact button group and clear active state.
layout.hero: App-like first viewport with product evidence, stats, and one primary action.

imagery.treatment: Clean, well-lit, minimally cropped. Avoid fake abstract dashboards.
imagery.crop: Frame interfaces straight-on with enough surrounding context to inspect them.

components.buttons: Small-radius buttons with icons for actions and clear focus rings.
components.cards: Use cards for discrete feature or metric units only, not whole page sections.
components.forms: Compact labels, helper text, visible validation, and generous hit targets.

themeJsonPresetHints: Register compact font sizes for labels, captions, and UI metadata.
Set contentSize around 720px and wideSize around 1180px.
Add subtle border and shadow custom tokens for reusable block styles.

accessibility: Maintain visible focus states on all utility controls.
Do not place tiny gray copy below 14px.
Use more than color for status badges.

styles.name: Neo Bauhaus Commerce
styles.summary: A geometric store style built from primary forms, rational grids, color blocking, and product-first composition.

palette.strategy: Warm neutral base plus saturated red, blue, and yellow used as structural color rather than decoration.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Carbon for body copy. Yellow needs dark text. Blue and red should be used with white or Studio White text only after testing.

typography.display: Geometric sans: Futura-like, Montserrat, Avenir Next, system-ui, sans-serif.
typography.body: Neutral sans with strong numerals for prices.
typography.accent: Wide uppercase sans for product labels and collection names.
typography.scale: Confident h1, compact h2, and strong product title sizes.
typography.usage: Mix regular and bold weights instead of using many font families.

layout.grid: Strict modular product grid with intentional color-block interruptions.
layout.rhythm: Even rhythm with occasional full-bleed bands.
layout.navigation: Horizontal nav with cart and search as icon-led utilities.
layout.hero: Collection hero with product photography sitting on geometric color fields.

imagery.treatment: Crisp shadows, clean backgrounds, and saturated color backdrops.
imagery.crop: Use square and 4:5 crops that align cleanly to the product grid.

components.buttons: Rectangular or pill-light buttons with strong color contrast and no ornamental shadows.
components.cards: Product cards are flat, grid-aligned, and image-dominant.
components.forms: Checkout and newsletter forms use boxed fields and clear step hierarchy.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create palette slugs that match color role and form names.
Add block styles for image-background product groups.
Set WooCommerce/product-grid equivalent blocks to use consistent image aspect ratios when available.

accessibility: Do not use color alone to distinguish sale or stock states.
Keep product text readable over color fields.
Use clear focus rings on cart and filter controls.

styles.name: Artisan Market
styles.summary: A warm handmade retail style with tactile surfaces, honest product photography, and gentle editorial pacing.

palette.strategy: Earthy base with clay, moss, and indigo accents to keep handmade from becoming beige-only.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Walnut Ink for text. Clay needs dark text when used as a pale surface or white text when darkened for buttons.

typography.display: Soft serif: Fraunces, Lora, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Humanist sans or readable serif depending on product density.
typography.accent: Small caps or mono for SKU, batch, origin, and maker labels.
typography.scale: Gentle editorial scale with medium h1 and generous paragraph leading.
typography.usage: Use serif headings for warmth and sans body for scannable shop flows.

layout.grid: Two to four-column product grids mixed with story-led full-width bands.
layout.rhythm: Breathing room between product groups and narrative sections.
layout.navigation: Simple shop categories with cart, search, and account utilities.
layout.hero: Seasonal product story with one strong photograph and understated copy.

imagery.treatment: Natural light, subtle grain, believable color, no heavy filters.
imagery.crop: Favor 4:5 and landscape process images with visible texture.

components.buttons: Soft rectangular buttons, not overly pill-shaped, with strong text contrast.
components.cards: Product cards can use paper-toned surfaces and simple captions.
components.forms: Friendly labels, large touch targets, and minimal checkout friction.

themeJsonPresetHints: Register palette colors as editor presets so makers can build seasonal pages consistently.
Define image block styles for framed, captioned, and full-bleed process photography.
Add paragraph and pullquote styles that support long product stories.

accessibility: Do not rely on low-contrast brown text.
Keep captions legible over textured backgrounds.
Make cart and checkout controls visually stronger than decorative links.

styles.name: Luxury Mono Gallery
styles.summary: A quiet premium style that uses restraint, precision, and image quality instead of decorative excess.

palette.strategy: Near-black and warm-white with a restrained metallic accent.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Obsidian or Soft Black for text. Gold is an accent, not body copy.

typography.display: High-contrast serif: Cormorant Garamond, Playfair Display, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Elegant sans: Neue Haas-style stack, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif.
typography.accent: Small uppercase tracking in the sans family for labels.
typography.scale: Large but composed h1, small metadata, and generous leading.
typography.usage: Headlines should be sparse; image captions and project metadata carry detail.

layout.grid: Wide single-image compositions with occasional two-column project details.
layout.rhythm: Slow vertical rhythm with deliberate pauses.
layout.navigation: Minimal wordmark and text links with subtle active states.
layout.hero: Full-bleed or near full-bleed image with restrained title placement.

imagery.treatment: Natural tonal depth, no heavy overlays, no generic blur.
imagery.crop: Let images breathe; avoid cropping away the object or space being sold.

components.buttons: Text-link buttons or thin-outline buttons with refined hover states.
components.cards: Avoid conventional cards; use image plus caption groupings.
components.forms: Minimal fields with clear labels and calm validation.

themeJsonPresetHints: Use wideSize generously, often 1320px or wider.
Create image block styles for edge-to-edge, inset, and captioned gallery modes.
Define custom spacing presets with large section gaps.

accessibility: Do not make navigation too faint.
Provide text alternatives for image-led project pages.
Ensure thin outline buttons have sufficient contrast.

styles.name: Civic Public Service
styles.summary: A clear, durable public-information style built for trust, accessibility, and fast wayfinding.

palette.strategy: Civic blue and green on a bright neutral base, with yellow reserved for alerts.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Civic Navy for body text. Alert Yellow needs dark text and should signal status only when paired with labels.

typography.display: Accessible sans: Atkinson Hyperlegible, Source Sans 3, Arial, sans-serif.
typography.body: Same family as display for consistency and readability.
typography.accent: Mono or tabular numerals for office hours, case numbers, and public data.
typography.scale: Practical scale with clear h1/h2 hierarchy and readable small text.
typography.usage: Favor clarity and scan speed over brand flourish.

layout.grid: Directory-first grid with prominent search and service cards.
layout.rhythm: Predictable spacing and visible section boundaries.
layout.navigation: Utility-heavy header with search, language, contact, and emergency links.
layout.hero: Task-oriented page intro with search or top services, not decorative messaging.

imagery.treatment: Honest, bright, documentary; icons must be simple and recognizable.
imagery.crop: Use practical crops that support orientation and service recognition.

components.buttons: High-contrast task buttons with clear verbs.
components.cards: Service cards with title, description, and action link; no vague teaser cards.
components.forms: Accessible labels, required field clarity, progress indicators, and error summary.

themeJsonPresetHints: Prioritize readable font sizes and strong link styles in global styles.
Add block styles for alert, service card, and document list groups.
Use patterns for reusable service-directory sections.

accessibility: Meet strong contrast targets by default.
Keep links visibly underlined in content.
Design forms with error summaries and field-level guidance.

styles.name: Retro Terminal
styles.summary: A code-forward visual style inspired by command lines, early computing, monospace rhythm, and phosphor glow restraint.

palette.strategy: Deep terminal background with green, amber, and cyan accents kept under control.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Phosphor for readable text, not neon green body copy. Glow effects should never reduce text clarity.

typography.display: Monospace display: IBM Plex Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace.
typography.body: Readable mono for short text or system sans for long docs.
typography.accent: Same mono family with tabular numerals and command-like prefixes.
typography.scale: Moderate scale with strong code block and preformatted text defaults.
typography.usage: Use mono for identity; switch long explanatory copy to sans if readability suffers.

layout.grid: Terminal-like panels arranged in responsive columns.
layout.rhythm: Consistent line-height rhythm that echoes code editors.
layout.navigation: Prompt-style links or compact top bar with status indicator.
layout.hero: Command output hero with project statement and quick-start commands.

imagery.treatment: Crisp, dark, minimal scanline or CRT texture only when subtle.
imagery.crop: Use horizontal screenshots and wide code panels that do not shrink text too far.

components.buttons: Terminal command buttons with brackets, carets, or compact icon labels.
components.cards: Panel cards with thin borders and header bars.
components.forms: Command-palette inspired inputs with strong caret and focus states.

themeJsonPresetHints: Style core/code and core/preformatted blocks as first-class visual elements.
Register dark palette presets and avoid relying on WordPress default colors.
Add block styles for terminal panel groups and command callouts.

accessibility: Avoid low-contrast neon on black for body text.
Do not animate cursor effects in a way that distracts from reading.
Provide light-mode or print-friendly variants for docs-heavy sites.

styles.name: Organic Wellness
styles.summary: A grounded wellness style that uses botanical color, gentle spacing, and calm but credible visual systems.

palette.strategy: Botanical greens and mineral neutrals with coral used sparingly for warmth.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Forest Ink for copy. Sage and Mineral are better as surfaces than small text colors.

typography.display: Soft serif or rounded humanist sans: Lora, Fraunces, Avenir, system-ui.
typography.body: Readable humanist sans with open counters.
typography.accent: Small serif italic or letter-spaced sans for quiet labels.
typography.scale: Moderate scale with relaxed line-height and few type sizes.
typography.usage: Keep type calm and professional; avoid overly spiritual decorative fonts.

layout.grid: Simple stacked sections with occasional two-column copy and image blocks.
layout.rhythm: Large vertical spacing and soft transitions between content types.
layout.navigation: Clear appointment or contact action in header.
layout.hero: Warm photo plus direct service promise and appointment path.

imagery.treatment: Soft natural light without hazy filters or vague spa imagery.
imagery.crop: Human-scale crops that create trust and avoid overused stock compositions.

components.buttons: Rounded but not bubbly, with enough weight to feel actionable.
components.cards: Gentle service cards with muted surfaces and clear outcomes.
components.forms: Spacious booking forms with reassuring copy and visible privacy hints.

themeJsonPresetHints: Define soft surface colors as palette presets for Group block backgrounds.
Add block styles for testimonials, service cards, and appointment callouts.
Use fluid spacing presets to create calm mobile layouts without huge gaps.

accessibility: Avoid pale green text on linen surfaces.
Keep booking actions prominent and keyboard reachable.
Make testimonial attribution clear.

styles.name: High Gloss Magazine
styles.summary: A fashion and culture magazine style with polished image direction, crisp typography, and confident editorial contrast.

palette.strategy: Black and white editorial base with one seasonal color and one soft surface tone.
palette.contrastNotes: Keep article text black on white. Seasonal colors work best for labels, issue covers, and hero accents.

typography.display: Fashion serif: Didot-like, Bodoni 72, Playfair Display, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Elegant but practical sans: Helvetica Neue, Arial, system-ui, sans-serif.
typography.accent: Condensed sans for departments, issue numbers, and image credits.
typography.scale: High contrast scale with enormous cover lines and restrained body copy.
typography.usage: Pair dramatic headlines with plain body text and disciplined captions.

layout.density: Varied, alternating sparse features and dense story grids.
layout.grid: Magazine grid with lead story, secondary stack, and column-based article packages.
layout.rhythm: Editorial pacing with image-led section openings.
layout.navigation: Department-driven nav with search and issue/archive access.
layout.hero: Cover-story composition with large image, cover line, deck, and issue label.

imagery.treatment: Polished, high-resolution, color-corrected, no dark unreadable overlays.
imagery.crop: Use deliberate magazine crops while preserving faces, products, and garment details.

components.buttons: Editorial text buttons or fine-outline buttons.
components.cards: Story cards prioritize image, title, department, and byline.
components.forms: Newsletter forms feel like subscription modules, not generic lead gen.

themeJsonPresetHints: Define custom post template styles for feature, review, and interview stories.
Style core/query blocks into lead story and magazine grid variations.
Add large display font-size presets but keep body defaults conservative.

accessibility: Avoid overlaying fine serif text on complex imagery.
Make bylines and dates readable, not decorative afterthoughts.
Provide visible keyboard focus in department navigation.

styles.name: Playful Maker Studio
styles.summary: A bright creator style that feels hands-on, energetic, and friendly without slipping into childish novelty.

palette.strategy: Creamy base with saturated but balanced studio colors.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Pencil for body copy. Lime is best as a surface or badge with dark text.

typography.display: Rounded sans: Nunito Sans, Baloo 2, Arial Rounded, system-ui, sans-serif.
typography.body: Friendly sans with clean readability.
typography.accent: Handwritten-looking accent only for small labels if legible.
typography.scale: Large friendly headings with compact project metadata.
typography.usage: Use accent typography sparingly; let color and layout create the play.

layout.grid: Responsive project grid with shape-like color bands and occasional offset cards.
layout.rhythm: Energetic sections separated by bold surface shifts.
layout.navigation: Simple nav with clear portfolio, shop, and booking paths.
layout.hero: Creator work visible immediately, with direct contact or shop action.

imagery.treatment: Bright, real, tactile, with occasional cutout treatment.
imagery.crop: Use varied aspect ratios but align them within a stable responsive grid.

components.buttons: Bold filled buttons with icon support and playful hover states.
components.cards: Project cards can use color backing but need stable dimensions.
components.forms: Workshop signup and contact forms with clear steps and warm validation messages.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create block styles for bright group backgrounds and playful project cards.
Register a medium-large border radius token while avoiding overly round cards.
Use pattern categories for portfolio, workshop, and shop sections.

accessibility: Ensure playful colors have enough contrast for real interface states.
Avoid text inside irregular shapes when it hurts readability.
Keep project card heights stable across viewport sizes.

styles.name: Minimalist Longform
styles.summary: A quiet reading-first style for essays, newsletters, and personal publishing where typography carries the experience.

palette.strategy: Warm paper and dark ink with one quiet link color and one note surface.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Reading Ink on Paper. Link Blue should be underlined in article bodies.

typography.display: Literary serif: Charter, Iowan Old Style, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Same serif stack for articles, optionally system sans for nav and metadata.
typography.accent: Small sans or mono for dates, reading time, and taxonomy.
typography.scale: Subtle scale with excellent paragraph rhythm and larger first-level headings.
typography.usage: Optimize body text before decorative decisions; line-height and measure are core.

layout.grid: Single-column articles with optional margin notes on wide screens.
layout.rhythm: Paragraph-first rhythm with clear breaks around quotes and lists.
layout.navigation: Quiet text navigation and simple archive access.
layout.hero: Publication title, concise description, and recent essays, not a marketing hero.

imagery.treatment: Use images sparingly and caption them well.
imagery.crop: Keep article images full-width within the reading measure or deliberately wide with captions.

components.buttons: Text links for most actions; simple filled button for subscribe.
components.cards: Archive cards should feel like bibliographic entries, not marketing cards.
components.forms: Newsletter signup with minimal fields and clear privacy note.

themeJsonPresetHints: Set contentSize between 680px and 760px for readable articles.
Style core/post-content, core/quote, and core/list carefully.
Add a style variation for dark reading mode only if contrast remains excellent.

accessibility: Keep body text at a comfortable size and measure.
Underline links in longform content.
Avoid faint metadata that becomes unreadable.
antiPatterns: decorative drop caps that break mobile flow

styles.name: Maximalist Collage
styles.summary: A layered cultural style made from cut paper, overlapping images, assertive headlines, and deliberately busy but controlled composition.

palette.strategy: Dark ink and paper base with hot campaign colors used as stickers and bands.
palette.contrastNotes: Keep long copy on Pasteup Paper or solid dark surfaces. Do not place body copy over busy collage.

typography.display: Chunky sans or slab: Cooper Black-like, Archivo Black, Arial Black, serif fallback.
typography.body: Plain sans for readable event and article content.
typography.accent: Mono or stamped sans for labels, dates, prices, and categories.
typography.scale: Wide scale with poster-size headlines and small label systems.
typography.usage: Contrast loud display type with very plain body copy.

layout.grid: Loose collage over an underlying grid so responsive layout remains stable.
layout.rhythm: Energetic section breaks with quiet content zones for details.
layout.navigation: Sticky, simple nav that anchors the busy page.
layout.hero: Layered poster composition with event or campaign signal visible immediately.

imagery.treatment: Layered, clipped, and tactile, but essential content remains readable.
imagery.crop: Use cutouts and irregular crops decoratively, not for critical product inspection.

components.buttons: Sticker-like action buttons with clear text and strong contrast.
components.cards: Event or article cards can overlap imagery but must keep text on solid surfaces.
components.forms: Ticket and signup forms should be calmer than the surrounding art direction.

themeJsonPresetHints: Register bright palette colors but keep body defaults neutral.
Use block patterns rather than arbitrary absolute positioning for collage sections.
Create safe text panel styles for content placed near layered media.

accessibility: Keep critical text out of textured backgrounds.
Do not make moving collage elements essential to understanding.
Give forms and purchase flows calmer, predictable layouts.

styles.name: Sports Card Americana
styles.summary: A nostalgic collectible-card style with vintage stats, team colors, textured borders, and playful identity systems.

palette.strategy: Cream stock and navy ink with red and field green accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use navy for copy. Red and green need careful contrast checks when placed over Card Stock.

typography.display: Athletic slab or varsity-adjacent serif: Graduate, Rockwell, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Plain sans for stats and descriptions.
typography.accent: Condensed sans or mono for card numbers, seasons, and stats.
typography.scale: Poster-like names, compact stats, readable collection pages.
typography.usage: Use display type for team identity and keep stats extremely legible.

layout.grid: Card grid with consistent aspect ratios and collection filters.
layout.rhythm: Alternates nostalgic feature areas with practical sortable indexes.
layout.navigation: Team, season, and archive navigation should be prominent.
layout.hero: Featured card or team roster with clear archive entry points.

imagery.treatment: Subtle print grain, aged stock, framed photography, and badge-like graphics.
imagery.crop: Consistent portrait and card aspect ratios matter more than decorative variation.

components.buttons: Pennant-like tabs or plain filled buttons with athletic color.
components.cards: Cards are the core object; preserve stable dimensions and back/front variants.
components.forms: Filter controls for team, season, type, and rarity.

themeJsonPresetHints: Define custom query card patterns for stable collectible grids.
Add table styles for stats with strong row and column readability.
Create style variations for baseball, basketball, and general team identities.

accessibility: Do not place small stat text on textured backgrounds.
Keep filters keyboard accessible and labeled.
Make color-coded teams distinguishable by text labels too.

styles.name: Mystic CMS Oracle
styles.summary: A playful occult-inspired style for weird technology, lore, cards, and narrative product experiences.

palette.strategy: Deep purple-black base with parchment, gold, and strange green accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use parchment for text on dark surfaces. Gold is best for borders and symbols, not paragraphs.

typography.display: Decorative serif used carefully: Cinzel, Cormorant Garamond, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Readable serif or sans depending on story length.
typography.accent: Small caps serif for card titles, numerals, and section labels.
typography.scale: Dramatic display titles with grounded body text.
typography.usage: Use ornament in titles and frames; keep instructions and UI plain.

layout.grid: Card grid with framed objects and ritual-like section sequencing.
layout.rhythm: Theatrical openings followed by scannable archive sections.
layout.navigation: Symbolic but labeled nav; never icon-only for core sections.
layout.hero: Large card, deck, or oracle object visible in the first viewport.

imagery.treatment: Illustrated or photographed with clear object detail; avoid muddy darkness.
imagery.crop: Use tall card ratios for featured artifacts and clean grids for archives.

components.buttons: Dark filled or gold-outline buttons with plain readable labels.
components.cards: Framed cards can be ornate, but inner content must stay readable.
components.forms: Subscription or reading forms use calm fields inside dramatic wrappers.

themeJsonPresetHints: Register dark and parchment palette colors, plus high-contrast link styles.
Add block styles for framed images, card grids, and symbolic dividers.
Use pattern categories for deck, reading, archive, and glossary.

accessibility: Do not use decorative symbols without text labels.
Keep ornate borders from crowding body text.
Avoid low-contrast gold text on purple backgrounds.

styles.name: Academic Library
styles.summary: A scholarly style that combines institutional clarity, citation-friendly typography, and calm research navigation.

palette.strategy: Ivory reading base with deep green, oxblood, and slate references.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Catalog Ink for text. Green and oxblood work for headings, rules, and filled controls with light text.

typography.display: Scholarly serif: Source Serif 4, Georgia, Iowan Old Style, serif.
typography.body: Readable serif for papers or sans for institutional pages.
typography.accent: Mono or tabular sans for identifiers, DOIs, dates, and catalog numbers.
typography.scale: Measured scale with strong h2/h3 distinction for structured documents.
typography.usage: Support citations, footnotes, abstracts, and metadata without visual clutter.

layout.grid: Main content plus metadata/sidebar on research pages.
layout.rhythm: Document-like rhythm with clear sectioning and anchor links.
layout.navigation: Search and collections navigation take priority over marketing links.
layout.hero: Catalog or research statement with search, collections, and recent publications.

imagery.treatment: Respect source material; use captions and provenance.
imagery.crop: Avoid crops that remove context from archival materials.

components.buttons: Plain institutional buttons with clear labels.
components.cards: Record cards show title, author, date, collection, and abstract excerpt.
components.forms: Search and filter forms are central, with precise labels.

themeJsonPresetHints: Style core/details for abstracts and citations.
Add block styles for metadata panels and document lists.
Define table styles for bibliographies and datasets.

accessibility: Keep search and filter controls well labeled.
Use semantic headings in long documents.
Ensure scanned materials have meaningful alt text or transcripts.

styles.name: Alpine Expedition
styles.summary: An outdoor style built from topographic structure, durable typography, and high-clarity adventure imagery.

palette.strategy: Alpine neutrals with evergreen, glacier blue, and safety orange accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Pine Black on Snow Field. Orange is excellent for alerts and route actions but needs dark text when used as a pale surface.

typography.display: Rugged sans or slab: Oswald, Roboto Condensed, Rockwell, system-ui.
typography.body: Clear sans for logistics and trip details.
typography.accent: Mono or condensed labels for elevation, distance, route, and weather stats.
typography.scale: Strong display headings with practical subheads and data labels.
typography.usage: Blend adventure personality with map-like information clarity.

layout.grid: Content grid supports maps, itinerary cards, gear lists, and images.
layout.rhythm: Section rhythm should feel like trip stages.
layout.navigation: Trips, routes, gear, journal, and booking paths are primary.
layout.hero: Landscape image or route map with immediate booking or guide action.

imagery.treatment: Crisp daylight, truthful weather, and high environmental detail.
imagery.crop: Use wide images for place, square cards for trips, and map crops for routes.

components.buttons: Durable filled buttons with icon support for booking and route actions.
components.cards: Trip cards show difficulty, duration, elevation, and location at a glance.
components.forms: Booking forms highlight dates, capacity, fitness level, and gear requirements.

themeJsonPresetHints: Add custom spacing presets for dense trip stats and broad image bands.
Create block styles for route facts, checklist groups, and alert bands.
Use query patterns for trip collections and journal posts.

accessibility: Do not encode difficulty only by color.
Keep route stats readable on mobile.
Provide alt text for maps and critical route imagery.
antiPatterns: dark scenic overlays behind small text

styles.name: Boutique Hotel
styles.summary: A hospitality style balancing immersive place imagery, clear booking paths, and refined local atmosphere.

palette.strategy: Warm neutral base with deep green, terracotta, and pool blue accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Charcoal Key for text. Terracotta and green can carry filled booking actions with light text if contrast passes.

typography.display: Elegant serif: Cormorant Garamond, Libre Baskerville, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Warm sans: Source Sans 3, system-ui, Arial, sans-serif.
typography.accent: Small uppercase sans for amenity labels and room metadata.
typography.scale: Graceful display scale with practical booking and room copy sizes.
typography.usage: Use serif for atmosphere, sans for logistics and booking clarity.

layout.grid: Room cards, amenity grids, local guide bands, and full-bleed place imagery.
layout.rhythm: Slow scenic sections interrupted by compact booking modules.
layout.navigation: Rooms, dining, experiences, location, and book action should always be findable.
layout.hero: Full-bleed place image with booking widget or clear reservation button visible.

imagery.treatment: Natural premium photography with place specificity and inspectable room details.
imagery.crop: Room images must show usable information, not only atmosphere.

components.buttons: Primary booking button should be stronger than secondary exploration links.
components.cards: Room cards include image, price hint, capacity, amenities, and action.
components.forms: Booking forms use clear dates, guests, availability, and errors.

themeJsonPresetHints: Register patterns for booking bars and room cards.
Create image styles for full-bleed, gallery, and room-card variants.
Set wideSize large enough for immersive image bands.

accessibility: Keep booking controls reachable and labeled.
Do not overlay small text on room photos.
Provide descriptive alt text for rooms and amenities.

styles.name: Food Zine
styles.summary: A lively culinary publishing style with recipe clarity, tactile food photography, and independent zine energy.

palette.strategy: Warm kitchen base with tomato, herb, and citrus accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Cast Iron for copy. Lemon needs dark text. Tomato works best for buttons and recipe labels with light text if contrast passes.

typography.display: Chunky serif or friendly slab: Cooper-like, Fraunces, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Readable sans or serif tuned for recipe steps.
typography.accent: Mono or condensed sans for prep time, yield, and ingredient amounts.
typography.scale: Expressive headlines with practical recipe body sizes.
typography.usage: Recipe instructions need more discipline than editorial features.

layout.grid: Recipe card grids, feature stories, and structured recipe templates.
layout.rhythm: Editorial energy on index pages, calm step-by-step rhythm on recipes.
layout.navigation: Search, cuisines, recipe types, and latest posts should be obvious.
layout.hero: Featured dish image with recipe metadata visible before scroll.

imagery.treatment: Natural appetizing color with texture; avoid gray or over-filtered food.
imagery.crop: Use vertical hero crops for dishes and step crops for process clarity.

components.buttons: Bold recipe action buttons for print, save, jump, and shop.
components.cards: Recipe cards show title, image, time, difficulty, and category.
components.forms: Search and dietary filters should be fast, visible, and well labeled.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create patterns for recipe facts and ingredient sections.
Style lists carefully for ingredients and numbered methods.
Add print-friendly styles for single recipe pages.

accessibility: Recipe steps must remain readable while cooking.
Use labels for dietary tags, not just colors.
Ensure jump links and print buttons are keyboard accessible.
antiPatterns: long personal intros before recipe controls

styles.name: Neon Night Market
styles.summary: A nocturnal event and commerce style with bright signage, dense discovery, and high-energy urban cues.

palette.strategy: Deep navy-black with cyan, pink, and citrus accents used like signage.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Sign White for body copy. Neon colors work for large labels, rules, and buttons but not long text.

typography.display: Condensed signage sans: Bebas Neue, Oswald, Impact, sans-serif.
typography.body: Clear sans with strong numerals for schedules and vendor info.
typography.accent: Mono or digital-like sans for times, booth numbers, and ticket codes.
typography.scale: Loud display scale with compact event metadata.
typography.usage: Make event logistics clearer than the nightlife styling.

layout.grid: Vendor and event cards in scrollable, filterable grids.
layout.rhythm: High-energy sections with strong anchors for schedule, map, and tickets.
layout.navigation: Tickets, vendors, schedule, map, and FAQ should be visible.
layout.hero: Night-market image or signage wall with immediate ticket or RSVP action.

imagery.treatment: Vibrant low-light photography without crushing all detail.
imagery.crop: Use wide crowd/context images and square vendor cards.

components.buttons: Bright filled buttons with dark text when using citrus or cyan.
components.cards: Vendor cards need photos, cuisine/product type, booth, and hours.
components.forms: Ticket forms are high contrast and calmer than the visual background.

themeJsonPresetHints: Add dark global styles and explicit button/link contrast tokens.
Create reusable schedule and vendor directory patterns.
Use query variations for vendor, event, and news indexes.

accessibility: Avoid flashing neon effects.
Ensure schedule and ticket info works without color cues.
Keep body copy bright enough against dark surfaces.

styles.name: Coastal Gallery
styles.summary: An airy art and lifestyle style that uses daylight, restraint, and coastal color without becoming nautical kitsch.

palette.strategy: Bright white and shell tones with sea glass, deep blue, and coral accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Deep Tide for text. Sea Glass and Dune are surfaces; do not use them for small text.

typography.display: Elegant light serif or refined sans: Canela-like, Cormorant, Avenir, system-ui.
typography.body: Readable sans with calm leading.
typography.accent: Small uppercase sans for artwork metadata and collection labels.
typography.scale: Gentle display scale and image-led hierarchy.
typography.usage: Let image spacing and captions carry refinement.

layout.grid: Gallery grid with consistent gaps and room for captions.
layout.rhythm: Slow daylight rhythm with soft section transitions.
layout.navigation: Collections, artists, shop, visit, and contact stay minimal but visible.
layout.hero: Large artwork or space image with understated title and visit or shop action.

imagery.treatment: Bright natural light, accurate color, no heavy blue filter.
imagery.crop: Artwork images should preserve full objects when selling or archiving.

components.buttons: Quiet outline or filled buttons with clear action labels.
components.cards: Artwork cards are image-first with title, artist, year, and status.
components.forms: Inquiry forms are short and respectful, with visible required fields.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create image block styles for artwork detail and gallery grid use.
Register caption and metadata type sizes.
Use query patterns for artist and artwork archives.

accessibility: Do not make captions too pale.
Provide complete alt text or artwork descriptions.
Keep inquiry actions visible without hovering.
antiPatterns: cropped artwork details as primary images

styles.name: Learning Lab
styles.summary: An optimistic education style with approachable color, clear lesson structure, and strong progress cues.

palette.strategy: Bright but not chaotic learning colors on a clean base.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Pencil Lead for text. Yellow needs dark text. Do not use color alone for progress.

typography.display: Rounded sans: Nunito, Atkinson Hyperlegible, system-ui, sans-serif.
typography.body: Accessible sans with generous line-height.
typography.accent: Mono or tabular sans for progress, scores, and step numbers.
typography.scale: Friendly headings and clear small labels.
typography.usage: Maintain grown-up clarity even when the audience is young.

layout.grid: Course, module, and lesson cards with visible status and sequence.
layout.rhythm: Chunked learning rhythm with summaries, exercises, and next actions.
layout.navigation: Course path, search, account, and progress access are primary.
layout.hero: Start-learning hero with course path or featured module visible.

imagery.treatment: Bright and specific, not generic stock classrooms.
imagery.crop: Use stable card crops and diagrams that remain readable on mobile.

components.buttons: Clear action buttons with icons for start, continue, complete, and download.
components.cards: Lesson cards show level, time, status, and next step.
components.forms: Quiz or signup forms need clear feedback and friendly errors.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create block styles for lesson cards and progress callouts.
Register bright palette colors but define accessible text pairings.
Use pattern categories for courses, exercises, resources, and profiles.

accessibility: Use readable type sizes for children and adults.
Give progress states text labels and ARIA-friendly structure.
Avoid overly animated learning feedback.

styles.name: Nonprofit Field Report
styles.summary: A mission-driven storytelling style with real-world evidence, donation clarity, and practical impact reporting.

palette.strategy: Humanitarian neutrals with trust blue, action orange, and field green.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Field Ink for most text. Action Orange should be reserved for donation and urgency states.

typography.display: Humanist sans or sturdy serif: Source Sans 3, Public Sans, Georgia, serif.
typography.body: Readable sans for reports and donation flows.
typography.accent: Tabular numerals for metrics and impact data.
typography.scale: Strong section headings, readable stories, large impact figures.
typography.usage: Use typography to separate story, evidence, and action.

layout.grid: Story modules, impact metrics, donation panels, and report sections.
layout.rhythm: Alternates human stories with proof and action.
layout.navigation: Donate, programs, impact, stories, and get involved stay visible.
layout.hero: Real field image plus direct mission statement and donation or volunteer action.

imagery.treatment: Respectful documentary photography with consent-conscious framing.
imagery.crop: Avoid exploitative close crops; provide context and dignity.

components.buttons: Donation action is prominent; secondary actions remain visible but quieter.
components.cards: Story cards include program, location, date, and clear read path.
components.forms: Donation and volunteer forms are transparent, short, and trust-building.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create strong button presets for donation actions.
Add block styles for impact metric groups and program story cards.
Use reusable patterns for report sections and calls to action.

accessibility: Ensure donation forms are accessible and transparent.
Use real text for impact numbers instead of image graphics.
Avoid color-only urgency cues.
antiPatterns: mission statements with no action path

styles.name: Creator Newsletter
styles.summary: A personality-led publishing style for creators who need subscription clarity, archive depth, and a strong voice.

palette.strategy: Clean reading base with one strong brand color and one warm subscribe accent.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Letter Ink for copy. Subscribe Coral should be tested for filled buttons and paired with light text only when darkened enough.

typography.display: Opinionated serif or clean sans depending on persona: Georgia, Charter, Inter, system-ui.
typography.body: Readable longform stack tuned for email-like clarity.
typography.accent: Mono or small sans for issue numbers and membership labels.
typography.scale: Article-friendly scale with a stronger homepage title.
typography.usage: Make the creator's voice the brand; avoid overly designed typography.

layout.grid: Homepage balances subscribe panel, latest issues, and topic archives.
layout.rhythm: Newsletter sections feel like issues and recurring columns.
layout.navigation: Start here, archive, topics, about, and subscribe.
layout.hero: Creator promise, author signal, and email capture visible immediately.

imagery.treatment: Personal and specific, not generic laptop stock.
imagery.crop: Use modest author images; do not let portraits dominate archive usability.

components.buttons: Subscribe button is primary and repeated at useful decision points.
components.cards: Issue cards show title, date, topic, and excerpt.
components.forms: Email signup forms are short, privacy-aware, and visually confident.

themeJsonPresetHints: Set strong paragraph and link styles for post content.
Create reusable subscribe and issue-list patterns.
Style query loops for compact chronological archives.

accessibility: Make signup forms obvious and labeled.
Avoid hiding archive access behind novelty layouts.
Use visible link states in long posts.

styles.name: Product Docs
styles.summary: A documentation style that prioritizes search, navigation, code readability, and task completion.

palette.strategy: Readable light interface with dark text, blue links, and subtle code surfaces.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Docs Ink on Docs White and Code Surface. Link Blue should remain visibly underlined or otherwise distinct.

typography.display: Neutral sans: Inter, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif.
typography.body: Same sans with strong line-height and readable small text.
typography.accent: Mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace.
typography.scale: Practical documentation scale with strong h2/h3 and compact code.
typography.usage: Code, tables, and step lists should receive as much attention as headings.

layout.grid: Sidebar navigation, main content, and optional in-page table of contents.
layout.rhythm: Task-based sections with short paragraphs and frequent anchors.
layout.navigation: Search, version switcher, docs sections, and next/previous links.
layout.hero: Docs home begins with search and common tasks, not a brand campaign.

imagery.treatment: Functional and inspectable, with annotations when helpful.
imagery.crop: Screenshots must retain relevant UI detail and not be overly cropped.

components.buttons: Utility buttons for copy, next, previous, and feedback use icons where appropriate.
components.cards: Task cards are compact and grouped by user goal.
components.forms: Search and feedback forms are short and highly accessible.

themeJsonPresetHints: Style core/code, core/preformatted, core/table, and core/details extensively.
Create narrow contentSize for docs pages with wideSize for examples.
Use block styles for note, warning, tip, and API reference panels.

accessibility: Make code copy controls keyboard accessible.
Do not rely on color only for callout type.
Keep navigation usable at high zoom.

styles.name: Festival Poster
styles.summary: A bold event style that turns lineups, dates, and ticketing into a poster-like system that still works as a website.

palette.strategy: Poster paper with saturated purple, orange, and mint for lineup energy.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Poster Ink for small text. Mint needs dark text; orange should be used in large areas or darkened for buttons.

typography.display: Condensed poster sans or expressive slab: Oswald, Archivo Black, Rockwell, sans-serif.
typography.body: Plain sans for logistics, FAQs, and ticket copy.
typography.accent: Mono or condensed sans for dates, venues, and pass types.
typography.scale: Very large lineup names, medium event details, compact metadata.
typography.usage: Protect schedule and ticket details from the poster energy.

layout.density: High on lineup sections, calmer on ticketing and FAQ.
layout.grid: Poster sections, lineup grids, day schedules, and ticket cards.
layout.rhythm: Rhythmic bands that feel like a stack of event posters.
layout.navigation: Lineup, schedule, tickets, venue, FAQ always visible.
layout.hero: Event name, dates, place, headliners, and ticket button in the first viewport.

imagery.treatment: High-energy color, sharp faces, and honest event context.
imagery.crop: Performer images need clear identification; lineup text must not depend on image readability.

components.buttons: Ticket buttons are bold, repeated, and separated from decorative poster labels.
components.cards: Artist and ticket cards use stable hierarchy and clear metadata.
components.forms: Newsletter and ticket forms are cleaner than poster sections.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create reusable lineup and schedule patterns with stable mobile behavior.
Register large display font sizes but pair with readable logistics styles.
Add color band group styles for day or stage sections.

accessibility: Do not use lineup type sizes so extreme that names wrap incoherently.
Keep schedule tables readable on mobile.
Ensure ticket actions are not hidden in poster art.
antiPatterns: overlapping text that breaks at mobile widths

styles.name: Financial Trust
styles.summary: A professional services style that signals stability, privacy, and clear decision support without looking generic.

palette.strategy: Deep navy and crisp white with green and copper accents for growth and warmth.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Trust Navy for text. Copper is for detail and calls to attention, not long text.

typography.display: Professional serif or sans: Source Serif 4, Merriweather, Inter, system-ui.
typography.body: Clear sans for service and compliance copy.
typography.accent: Tabular numerals for data, rates, and financial figures.
typography.scale: Conservative scale with readable service pages and strong callouts.
typography.usage: Use restraint; credibility comes from clarity and consistency.

layout.grid: Service pathway cards, proof bands, article lists, and consultation forms.
layout.rhythm: Predictable sections with high trust and low surprise.
layout.navigation: Services, who we help, resources, about, and consultation action.
layout.hero: Clear audience and outcome statement with consultation path and trust marker.

imagery.treatment: Professional, bright, specific, and not staged handshake stock.
imagery.crop: Prioritize faces, team credibility, and context over abstract finance imagery.

components.buttons: Consultation buttons are clear, calm, and repeated at decision points.
components.cards: Service cards communicate audience, problem, and next step.
components.forms: Forms feel secure, minimal, and explicit about privacy.

themeJsonPresetHints: Style buttons and forms for confidence rather than flash.
Create reusable service, proof, and advisor profile patterns.
Set table styles for comparison and resource pages.

accessibility: Avoid low-contrast gray body copy.
Make privacy and compliance notes readable.
Use clear labels and validation on inquiry forms.

styles.name: Architectural Atelier
styles.summary: A disciplined architecture style with plan-like grids, project photography, and precise technical storytelling.

palette.strategy: Drawing-board neutrals with blueprint blue and material rust accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Graphite Line for text. Concrete Warm is a surface, not a text color.

typography.display: Precise sans: Neue Haas-style, Helvetica Neue, Inter, Arial, sans-serif.
typography.body: Readable sans with strong metadata treatment.
typography.accent: Mono for project facts, coordinates, dimensions, and drawing labels.
typography.scale: Restrained headings and crisp project titles.
typography.usage: Use alignment, measure, and metadata instead of decorative typography.

layout.grid: Project grid, plan-like two-column details, and full-width image sequences.
layout.rhythm: Gallery-like pacing with precise metadata breaks.
layout.navigation: Projects, practice, writing, studio, contact.
layout.hero: Project image or studio statement with visible project index link.

imagery.treatment: Accurate color, strong geometry, no heavy filters.
imagery.crop: Respect spatial context; avoid crops that make architecture unreadable.

components.buttons: Understated text links or thin-outline controls.
components.cards: Project cards show image, type, location, year, and status.
components.forms: Inquiry forms are minimal but clear about project scope.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create project archive query patterns with consistent image ratios.
Define mono metadata font styles and small caption presets.
Add image styles for plans, details, and full-bleed project photography.

accessibility: Do not make metadata so small it becomes decorative.
Provide text descriptions for plans and diagrams.
Keep thin-line UI controls visible.

styles.name: Vintage Newspaper
styles.summary: A newsprint-inspired publication style with columns, mastheads, bylines, and archival warmth.

palette.strategy: Aged paper and dark ink with muted blue and red section markers.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Printer Ink for body copy. Muted section colors should label content, not replace readable text.

typography.display: Newspaper serif: Georgia, Libre Baskerville, Times New Roman, serif.
typography.body: Readable serif with strong line-height, not cramped historical mimicry.
typography.accent: Condensed sans or small caps for departments, dates, and labels.
typography.scale: Masthead-sized title, strong article heads, compact bylines.
typography.usage: Reference newspapers without reproducing inaccessible dense print.

layout.grid: Responsive newspaper columns that collapse cleanly on mobile.
layout.rhythm: Rules, departments, and bylines structure the page.
layout.navigation: Sections, latest, archive, search, and submit tips.
layout.hero: Masthead and lead story package rather than a generic hero.

imagery.treatment: Subtle grain and captions; preserve historical context.
imagery.crop: Do not crop archival images in ways that distort their meaning.

components.buttons: Plain text or boxed buttons styled like classifieds.
components.cards: Story cards resemble article packages with byline, section, and excerpt.
components.forms: Search and tip forms need modern usability under vintage styling.

themeJsonPresetHints: Style query loops into lead story and section package patterns.
Add global styles for captions, bylines, and separators.
Create archive templates that emphasize date and section browsing.

accessibility: Do not make columns too narrow for modern reading.
Keep archive search prominent.
Use captions and alt text for historical images.
antiPatterns: fake paper stains behind body copy

styles.name: Translucent AI
styles.summary: A contemporary AI/product style using luminous surfaces, clear product evidence, and restrained translucency.

palette.strategy: Cool light base with deep ink, electric blue, violet, and mint accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Deep Interface for text. Translucent surfaces must have solid fallback backgrounds and tested contrast.

typography.display: Modern sans: Inter, Satoshi-like, system-ui, sans-serif.
typography.body: Crisp sans with readable paragraphs and product UI labels.
typography.accent: Mono for model names, parameters, and technical metrics.
typography.scale: Clear product scale with strong h1 and compact proof modules.
typography.usage: Make the product feel current without replacing clarity with gloss.

layout.grid: Product panels, use-case grids, workflow diagrams, and proof sections.
layout.rhythm: Evidence-led sections that alternate screenshot, benefit, and detail.
layout.navigation: Product, use cases, docs, pricing, and contact or start action.
layout.hero: Actual product state or workflow visible immediately, with claims grounded nearby.

imagery.treatment: Sharp, inspectable, not dark blurred abstract shapes.
imagery.crop: Screenshots must remain legible on desktop and mobile previews.

components.buttons: Crisp filled buttons with icons for start, demo, and docs.
components.cards: Use translucent panels sparingly over solid surfaces, never over complex imagery.
components.forms: Demo forms should be compact, high contrast, and trust-building.

themeJsonPresetHints: Define gradient presets only for large surfaces, not text.
Add block styles for product panels and metric strips.
Register mono font presets for technical metadata.

accessibility: Do not put small text on glass effects without solid contrast.
Avoid motion that implies intelligence but obscures product details.
Label diagrams and screenshots clearly.

styles.name: Neighborhood Restaurant
styles.summary: A practical local restaurant style that foregrounds menu, hours, reservations, and appetizing real photography.

palette.strategy: Warm dining-room base with chalk, tomato, and bottle green accents.
palette.contrastNotes: Use Chalkboard for text. Butter requires dark text. Tomato is strong for reservations and specials.

typography.display: Warm serif or hand-painted sans used carefully: Cooper-like, Fraunces, Georgia, sans-serif.
typography.body: Clear sans for menu, hours, ordering, and contact.
typography.accent: Mono or tabular numerals for prices, hours, and phone numbers.
typography.scale: Readable practical scale with a characterful restaurant name.
typography.usage: Menu readability beats decorative restaurant atmosphere.

layout.grid: Menu sections, photo bands, reservation callouts, and location details.
layout.rhythm: Practical top sections followed by atmosphere and story.
layout.navigation: Menu, reservations, order, hours, location, events.
layout.hero: Food or dining-room image with menu and reservation actions immediately visible.

imagery.treatment: Real, appetizing, well-lit, and specific to the restaurant.
imagery.crop: Show food clearly; avoid moody crops that obscure the dish.

components.buttons: Reservation and order buttons are clear, repeated, and mobile-friendly.
components.cards: Menu cards or sections show item, description, price, and dietary labels.
components.forms: Reservation forms are short, direct, and error-resistant.

themeJsonPresetHints: Create reusable menu, hours, and reservation patterns.
Style tables and lists for menus without relying on image PDFs.
Use button presets for reservation, order, and call actions.

accessibility: Do not use PDF-only menus.
Keep phone, hours, address, and reservation controls easy to find.
Label dietary and allergen information with text, not just icons.
antiPatterns: full-screen atmospheric hero with no menu link
